name: fetchRP
code:
1	/**
2	 * @description This file is a modified version of `fetch-result-please` (`ofetch`), minimalized and adapted to Hono's custom needs.
3	 *
4	 * @link https://www.npmjs.com/package/fetch-result-please
5	 */
6	
7	const nullBodyResponses = new Set([101, 204, 205, 304])
8	
9	/**
10	 * Smartly parses and return the consumable result from a fetch `Response`.
11	 *
12	 * Throwing a structured error if the response is not `ok`. ({@link DetailedError})
13	 */
14	export async function fetchRP(fetchRes: Response | Promise<Response>): Promise<any> {
15	  const _fetchRes = (await fetchRes) as unknown as Response & {
16	    _data: any
17	    /**
18	     * @description BodyInit property from whatwg-fetch polyfill
19	     *
20	     * @link https://github.com/JakeChampion/fetch/blob/main/fetch.js#L238
21	     */
22	    _bodyInit?: any
23	  }
24	
25	  const hasBody =
26	    (_fetchRes.body || _fetchRes._bodyInit) && !nullBodyResponses.has(_fetchRes.status)
27	
28	  if (hasBody) {
29	    const responseType = detectResponseType(_fetchRes)
30	    _fetchRes._data = await _fetchRes[responseType]()
31	  }
32	
33	  if (!_fetchRes.ok) {
34	    throw new DetailedError(`${_fetchRes.status} ${_fetchRes.statusText}`, {
35	      statusCode: _fetchRes?.status,
36	      detail: {
37	        data: _fetchRes?._data,
38	        statusText: _fetchRes?.statusText,
39	      },
40	    })
41	  }
42	
43	  return _fetchRes._data
44	}

35	      statusCode: _fetchRes?.status,
36	      detail: {
37	        data: _fetchRes?._data,
38	        statusText: _fetchRes?.statusText,
39	      },
40	    })

44	}
45	
46	export class DetailedError extends Error {
47	  /**
48	   * Additional `message` that will be logged AND returned to client
49	   */
50	  public detail?: any
51	  /**
52	   * Additional `code` that will be logged AND returned to client
53	   */
54	  public code?: any
55	  /**
56	   * Additional value that will be logged AND NOT returned to client
57	   */
58	  public log?: any
59	  /**
60	   * Optionally set the status code to return, in a web server context
61	   */
62	  public statusCode?: any
63	
64	  constructor(
65	    message: string,
66	    options: { detail?: any; code?: any; statusCode?: number; log?: any } = {}
67	  ) {
68	    super(message)
69	    this.name = 'DetailedError'
70	    this.log = options.log
71	    this.detail = options.detail
72	    this.code = options.code
73	    this.statusCode = options.statusCode
74	  }
75	}

name: jsonRegex
code:
58	  public log?: any
59	  /**
60	   * Optionally set the status code to return, in a web server context
61	   */
62	  public statusCode?: any
63	
64	  constructor(
65	    message: string,
66	    options: { detail?: any; code?: any; statusCode?: number; log?: any } = {}
67	  ) {
68	    super(message)
69	    this.name = 'DetailedError'
70	    this.log = options.log
71	    this.detail = options.detail
72	    this.code = options.code
73	    this.statusCode = options.statusCode
74	  }
75	}
76	
77	// This is used to match the content-type header for 'json'
78	const jsonRegex = /^application\/(?:[\w!#$%&*.^`~-]*\+)?json(?:;.+)?$/i
79	
80	function detectResponseType(response: Response): 'json' | 'text' {
81	  const _contentType = response.headers.get('content-type')
82	
83	  if (!_contentType) {
84	    return 'text'
85	  }
86	
87	  // `_contentType` might look like: `application/json; charset=utf-8`, `text/plain`, so we get the first part before `;`
88	  const contentType = _contentType.split(';').shift()!
89	
90	  if (jsonRegex.test(contentType)) {
91	    return 'json'
92	  }
93	
94	  return 'text'
95	}

77	// This is used to match the content-type header for 'json'
78	const jsonRegex = /^application\/(?:[\w!#$%&*.^`~-]*\+)?json(?:;.+)?$/i
79	
80	function detectResponseType(response: Response): 'json' | 'text' {
81	  const _contentType = response.headers.get('content-type')
82	
83	  if (!_contentType) {
84	    return 'text'
85	  }
86	
87	  // `_contentType` might look like: `application/json; charset=utf-8`, `text/plain`, so we get the first part before `;`
88	  const contentType = _contentType.split(';').shift()!
89	
90	  if (jsonRegex.test(contentType)) {
91	    return 'json'
92	  }
93	
94	  return 'text'
95	}

name: getColorEnabled
code:
1	/**
2	 * @module
3	 * Color utility.
4	 */
5	
6	/**
7	 * Get whether color change on terminal is enabled or disabled.
8	 * If `NO_COLOR` environment variable is set, this function returns `false`.
9	 * Unlike getColorEnabledAsync(), this cannot check Cloudflare environment variables.
10	 * @see {@link https://no-color.org/}
11	 *
12	 * @returns {boolean}
13	 */
14	export function getColorEnabled(): boolean {
15	  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
16	  const { process, Deno } = globalThis as any
17	
18	  const isNoColor =
19	    typeof Deno?.noColor === 'boolean'
20	      ? (Deno.noColor as boolean)
21	      : process !== undefined
22	        ? // eslint-disable-next-line no-unsafe-optional-chaining
23	          'NO_COLOR' in process?.env
24	        : false
25	
26	  return !isNoColor
27	}

21	      : process !== undefined

28	
29	/**
30	 * Get whether color change on terminal is enabled or disabled.
31	 * If `NO_COLOR` environment variable is set, this function returns `false`.
32	 * @see {@link https://no-color.org/}
33	 *
34	 * @returns {boolean}
35	 */
36	export async function getColorEnabledAsync(): Promise<boolean> {
37	  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
38	  const { navigator } = globalThis as any
39	  // Avoid analysis of cloudflare scheme by bundlers
40	  const cfWorkers = 'cloudflare:workers'
41	
42	  const isNoColor =
43	    navigator !== undefined && navigator.userAgent === 'Cloudflare-Workers'
44	      ? await (async () => {
45	          try {
46	            // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/ban-ts-comment
47	            // @ts-ignore
48	            return 'NO_COLOR' in ((await import(cfWorkers)).env ?? {}) // {} is for backward compat
49	          } catch {
50	            return false
51	          }
52	        })()
53	      : !getColorEnabled()
54	
55	  return !isNoColor
56	}
